question: Design a `Playlist` class that contains a dynamically allocated array of `Song` objects. The `Song` class has `title` and `artist`. `Playlist` should manage adding and removing songs (demonstrating composition with dynamic memory).

test_cases.input:
add Song A;Artist X
add Song B;Artist Y
print
copy_test
print
test_cases.expected:
Playlist:
"Song A" by Artist X
"Song B" by Artist Y
Copied Playlist:
Playlist:
"Song A" by Artist X
"Song B" by Artist Y
Playlist:
"Song A" by Artist X
"Song B" by Artist Y

test_cases.input:
add S1;A1
add S2;A2
copy_test
remove S1;A1
print
test_cases.expected:
Playlist:
"S1" by A1
"S2" by A2
Copied Playlist:
Playlist:
"S1" by A1
"S2" by A2
Playlist:
"S2" by A2

driver: std::string command, title, artist; Playlist myPlaylist; while (std::cin >> command) { if (command == "add") { std::cin.ignore(); std::getline(std::cin, title, ';'); std::getline(std::cin, artist); myPlaylist.addSong(Song(title, artist)); } else if (command == "remove") { std::cin.ignore(); std::getline(std::cin, title, ';'); std::getline(std::cin, artist); myPlaylist.removeSong(Song(title, artist)); } else if (command == "print") { myPlaylist.printPlaylist(); } else if (command == "copy_test") { Playlist copiedPlaylist = myPlaylist; std::cout << "Copied Playlist:\n"; copiedPlaylist.printPlaylist(); } }
errors: The `Playlist` class is missing a user-defined copy constructor and a copy assignment operator. Since `Playlist` manages a dynamically allocated array (`_songs`), the implicitly generated copy operations perform shallow copies. This means that when a `Playlist` object is copied, both the original and the copy point to the same underlying array. This leads to a double-free error or memory corruption when either the original or the copied object is destroyed, as both will attempt to `delete[]` the same memory. This violates the Rule of Three/Five for classes managing dynamic resources.
